result.markdown:
---
title: "Replication: Bloom (2009)"
subtitle: "The Impact of Uncertainty Shocks"
---



## Overview

This document replicates the main empirical results of @bloom2009, which identifies uncertainty shocks using a Cholesky decomposition. The uncertainty indicator â€” constructed from 17 identified high-volatility events â€” is ordered first so that it has a contemporaneous effect on all other variables but is itself unaffected by them within the month.

**Reference:** Bloom, N. (2009). The impact of uncertainty shocks. *Econometrica*, 77(3), 623â€“685.

---

## Setup


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
library(tidyverse)
library(tidyMacro)
library(tictoc)

set_theme(ftheme_tidyMacro())
```
:::


---

## Data


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
data("Bloom2009")

dates_vec <- Bloom2009 |> pull(Date)
y         <- Bloom2009 |> select(-Date) |> as.matrix()

T <- nrow(y)
N <- ncol(y)

var_names <- colnames(y)
shockname <- "UNCERT"
shock     <- match(shockname, var_names)
```
:::


---

## VAR Estimation


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
p <- 12   # lag length used in the original paper
c <- 1

var_bloom <- fVAR(y, p, c)
sigma     <- var_bloom$sigma_full

# Cholesky factor (lower triangular)
S <- t(chol(sigma))

# Wold IRFs
horizon   <- 48
wold      <- fwoldIRF(var_bloom, horizon = horizon)
point_irf <- fcholeskyIRF(wold, S)
```
:::


---

## Bootstrap Confidence Bands

### Standard Bootstrap


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
tic()
bloom_chol <- fbootstrapChol(
    y          = y,
    var_result = var_bloom,
    nboot      = 1000,
    horizon    = horizon,
    prc        = 68,
    bootscheme = "residual"
)
toc()
#> 16.609 sec elapsed
```
:::


### Bias-Corrected Bootstrap


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
tic()
bloom_corrected <- fbootstrapCholCorrected(
    y          = y,
    var_result = var_bloom,
    nboot1     = 1000,
    nboot2     = 2000,
    horizon    = horizon,
    prc        = 68,
    bootscheme = "residual"
)
toc()
#> 39.265 sec elapsed
```
:::


---

## Impulse Response Functions

### Standard Bootstrap


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
fplotirf_chol(
    point_irf,
    bloom_chol$upper,
    bloom_chol$lower,
    shock    = shock,
    varnames = var_names,
    prc      = 68,
    facet_ncol = 4
)
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![IRFs to uncertainty shock â€” standard bootstrap (68% confidence bands)](Bloom2009_files/figure-html/fig-irf-standard-1.png){#fig-irf-standard width=100%}
:::
:::


### Bias-Corrected Bootstrap


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
fplotirf_chol(
    point_irf,
    bloom_corrected$upper,
    bloom_corrected$lower,
    shock    = shock,
    varnames = var_names,
    prc      = 68,
    facet_ncol = 4
)
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![IRFs to uncertainty shock â€” bias-corrected bootstrap (68% confidence bands)](Bloom2009_files/figure-html/fig-irf-corrected-1.png){#fig-irf-corrected width=100%}
:::
:::


---

## Forecast Error Variance Decomposition


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
vardec <- fevd_chol(point_irf, shock = shock)

fplot_vardec(
    fevd       = vardec$fevd,
    varnames   = var_names,
    shocknames = shockname
)
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![Forecast error variance decomposition: share explained by uncertainty shock](Bloom2009_files/figure-html/fig-fevd-1.png){#fig-fevd width=100%}
:::
:::


---

## Historical Decomposition


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
# Exclude the shock variable itself from the response variables
series <- setdiff(seq_len(N), shock)

histdec_list <- setNames(
    lapply(series, function(i) fhistdec(y, var_bloom, S, i)$histdec),
    colnames(y)[series]
)

fplot_histdec(
    histdec_list = histdec_list,
    shock        = shock,
    shockname    = shockname,
    dates        = dates_vec,
    p            = p,
    facet_ncol   = 2
) +
    scale_x_date(date_breaks = "7 years", date_labels = "%Y")
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![Historical decomposition: contribution of uncertainty shock](Bloom2009_files/figure-html/fig-histdec-1.png){#fig-histdec width=100%}
:::
:::
